title: What's Included in a Professional Website Design Package?
excerpt: Not all website design packages are equal — and comparing quotes without understanding what's included is a recipe for confusion and disappointment. This guide breaks down what a comprehensive professional website design package should include, so you know exactly what to look for.
content:
When you receive a proposal for a website design project, the line items can be confusing. What exactly are you paying for? What should be standard, and what's legitimately extra? Here's your guide.

What a Professional Website Design Package Should Include

Discovery and Strategy A kickoff meeting, business/goal analysis, competitor review, and agreement on site structure. This sets the strategic foundation for everything that follows. Site Architecture / Information Architecture A documented structure of all pages, their hierarchy, and how they interlink. This is the blueprint before any design begins. Wireframes Low-fidelity layouts mapping out content placement and page structure. Essential for getting structural feedback before committing to visual design. Responsive Visual Design High-fidelity design of all page templates in desktop and mobile views. This is the visual layer — colors, typography, imagery, layout. Design Revisions A defined number of revision rounds should be included. Typically 2–3 rounds per design phase is industry standard. Front-End Development Coding the designs into a functional website using HTML/CSS/JS and a CMS (like WordPress, Webflow, or a custom framework). CMS Setup and Training Setting up the content management system and training your team on how to update and manage content after launch. Basic On-Page SEO Meta titles, meta descriptions, proper heading structure, image alt text, and XML sitemap submission. Testing and QA Cross-browser and cross-device testing, performance testing, and bug fixing. Launch Support DNS migration and going live.

What's Often an Extra Cost

  • Copywriting and content creation
  • Photography and custom illustration
  • Advanced e-commerce functionality
  • Third-party integrations (CRM, payment gateways, booking systems)
  • Ongoing maintenance and hosting
  • Advanced SEO strategy

What Bluewaves Technology Delivers
